title: GIZ and Thyssenkrupp Nucera Collaborate to Boost India's Green Hydrogen Industry
content:
Deutsche Gesellschaft fΟÉ¬Φr Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH and thyssenkrupp nucera have entered into a new cooperation to accelerate the development of green hydrogen and Power-to-X (PtX) markets in India. Announced at India Energy Week in Goa, the partnership brings together international development cooperation and private-sector technological expertise to unlock opportunities along the countryΟΔβ²§β³Δs hydrogen value chain.
The partnership is implemented under the International Hydrogen Ramp-Up (H2Uppp) program. H2Uppp is supporting entrepreneurial engagement in the ramping-up of hydrogen in the Global South and is a funding programme of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy (BMWE) and is implemented by GIZ. Within this framework, GIZ ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a federal enterprise specializing in international cooperation for sustainable development ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ is responsible for implementation, while thyssenkrupp nucera ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a global leader in electrolysis technology ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ contributes its technological and global hydrogen market expertise.

ΟΔβ²§≈™India is one of the most promising future markets for green hydrogen electrolysis. This cooperation enables us to deepen our understanding of the local market and engage more closely with IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs hydrogen ecosystem. It also reflects our strong commitment to supporting IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission,ΟΔβ²§¬ù says Kiran Paul Joseph, CEO of thyssenkrupp nucera India. ΟΔβ²§≈™With its strong network, deep understanding of the local context and long-standing experience in international cooperation, GIZ is an excellent partner for this next step.ΟΔβ²§¬ù
ΟΔβ²§≈™German companies such as thyssenkrupp nucera bring substantial expertise in electrolyzer technologies, hydrogen infrastructure, and large-scale Power-to-X project development to the global market,ΟΔβ²§¬ù says Gunther Grathwohl, Head of Division for International Ramp-up and Bilateral Hydrogen Infrastructure Cooperation, BMWE. ΟΔβ²§≈™The H2Uppp cooperation links this German private-sector know-how with IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs rapid industrial growth and strong renewable energy potential to accelerate green hydrogen markets and advance the global energy transition. The cooperation also creates strategic perspectives for the German industry in emerging green hydrogen markets.ΟΔβ²§¬ù

ΟΔβ²§≈™Green hydrogen offers significant potential for sustainable economic development and climate-friendly industrial growth in India,ΟΔβ²§¬ù says Regine Dietz, Project Director, H2Uppp, GIZ. ΟΔβ²§≈™Through this cooperation, we aim to connect German expertise with local stakeholders to help translate this potential into greater market transparency and a clearer understanding of future development pathways, supporting IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs ambition to become a global green hydrogen hub.ΟΔβ²§¬ù

As one of the worldΟΔβ²§β³Δs fastest-growing economies, India is expected to play a central role in the global energy transition. The partnership focuses on a detailed assessment of IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs emerging green hydrogen landscape. This includes leveraging well-established market potential, identifying strategic areas for intensified collaboration with relevant stakeholders, and supporting further market development, contributing to the growth of a sustainable and competitive green hydrogen economy in India. In addition, the partnership will explore opportunities for scalable deployment models that could support costΟΔβ²§βÄ‰efficient project implementation.
This analytical work builds on thyssenkrupp nuceraΟΔβ²§β³Δs strong industrial presence in IndiaΟΔβ²§β³Δs chlor-alkali market, where the electrolysis specialist holds a leading position. This long-standing footprint provides a solid foundation for expanding activities in the countryΟΔβ²§β³Δs emerging green hydrogen sector.
